title.he: The Path of Renunciation of Action
title.ar: The Path of Renunciation of Action
title.tr: The Path of Renunciation of Action
title.sw: The Path of Renunciation of Action

he.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa you speak of renunciation of actions and then again You speak of the path of selfless action Therefore please tell me clearly which is superior

ar.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa you speak of renunciation of actions and then again You speak of the path of selfless action Therefore please tell me clearly which is superior

tr.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa you speak of renunciation of actions and then again You speak of the path of selfless action Therefore please tell me clearly which is superior

sw.text: Arjuna said O Kṛṣṇa you speak of renunciation of actions and then again You speak of the path of selfless action Therefore please tell me clearly which is superior

he.text: The Supreme Lord said Both renunciation of action and the path of selfless action bring great benefit Yet of the two know that the way of selfless action is superior

ar.text: The Supreme Lord said Both renunciation of action and the path of selfless action bring great benefit Yet of the two know that the way of selfless action is superior

tr.text: The Supreme Lord said Both renunciation of action and the path of selfless action bring great benefit Yet of the two know that the way of selfless action is superior

sw.text: The Supreme Lord said Both renunciation of action and the path of selfless action bring great benefit Yet of the two know that the way of selfless action is superior

he.text: O mighty-armed Arjuna know that one who is free from attraction and aversion is truly renounced though performing action for one who is free from duality easily attains liberation from bondage

ar.text: O mighty-armed Arjuna know that one who is free from attraction and aversion is truly renounced though performing action for one who is free from duality easily attains liberation from bondage

tr.text: O mighty-armed Arjuna know that one who is free from attraction and aversion is truly renounced though performing action for one who is free from duality easily attains liberation from bondage

sw.text: O mighty-armed Arjuna know that one who is free from attraction and aversion is truly renounced though performing action for one who is free from duality easily attains liberation from bondage

he.text: The path of renunciation sāṅkhya-yoga and the path of selfless action karma-yoga are considered separate paths by the unwise but not by the learned One who perfectly follows either of these paths will achieve the result of both

ar.text: The path of renunciation sāṅkhya-yoga and the path of selfless action karma-yoga are considered separate paths by the unwise but not by the learned One who perfectly follows either of these paths will achieve the result of both

tr.text: The path of renunciation sāṅkhya-yoga and the path of selfless action karma-yoga are considered separate paths by the unwise but not by the learned One who perfectly follows either of these paths will achieve the result of both

sw.text: The path of renunciation sāṅkhya-yoga and the path of selfless action karma-yoga are considered separate paths by the unwise but not by the learned One who perfectly follows either of these paths will achieve the result of both

he.text: The state achieved by the renunciation of action is also achieved by the performance of selfless action One who sees these two paths to be one and the same actually sees

ar.text: The state achieved by the renunciation of action is also achieved by the performance of selfless action One who sees these two paths to be one and the same actually sees

tr.text: The state achieved by the renunciation of action is also achieved by the performance of selfless action One who sees these two paths to be one and the same actually sees

sw.text: The state achieved by the renunciation of action is also achieved by the performance of selfless action One who sees these two paths to be one and the same actually sees

he.text: O mighty hero without selfless action mere renunciation of action is the cause of sorrow But the wise man who engages in selfless action swiftly attains to the Absolute

ar.text: O mighty hero without selfless action mere renunciation of action is the cause of sorrow But the wise man who engages in selfless action swiftly attains to the Absolute

tr.text: O mighty hero without selfless action mere renunciation of action is the cause of sorrow But the wise man who engages in selfless action swiftly attains to the Absolute

sw.text: O mighty hero without selfless action mere renunciation of action is the cause of sorrow But the wise man who engages in selfless action swiftly attains to the Absolute

he.text: The clean-hearted person thus engaged in yoga controls his mind and senses Becoming filled with affection and compassion for all beings although fully active he is never implicated by any action

ar.text: The clean-hearted person thus engaged in yoga controls his mind and senses Becoming filled with affection and compassion for all beings although fully active he is never implicated by any action

tr.text: The clean-hearted person thus engaged in yoga controls his mind and senses Becoming filled with affection and compassion for all beings although fully active he is never implicated by any action

sw.text: The clean-hearted person thus engaged in yoga controls his mind and senses Becoming filled with affection and compassion for all beings although fully active he is never implicated by any action

he.text: One who though living in this world selflessly offers all his actions to the Supreme Lord is not polluted by sin as a lotus leaf remains on the water yet is untouched by it

ar.text: One who though living in this world selflessly offers all his actions to the Supreme Lord is not polluted by sin as a lotus leaf remains on the water yet is untouched by it

tr.text: One who though living in this world selflessly offers all his actions to the Supreme Lord is not polluted by sin as a lotus leaf remains on the water yet is untouched by it

sw.text: One who though living in this world selflessly offers all his actions to the Supreme Lord is not polluted by sin as a lotus leaf remains on the water yet is untouched by it

he.text: By giving up attachment the karma-yogīs perform action merely through the body mind intelligence and even the senses for self-purification

ar.text: By giving up attachment the karma-yogīs perform action merely through the body mind intelligence and even the senses for self-purification

tr.text: By giving up attachment the karma-yogīs perform action merely through the body mind intelligence and even the senses for self-purification

sw.text: By giving up attachment the karma-yogīs perform action merely through the body mind intelligence and even the senses for self-purification

he.text: Giving up attachment to the fruits of his actions the materially unmotivated niṣkām karma-yogī attains constant שלום or liberation from actions and reactions But the fruitive worker sakām karmī is attached to the fruits of his actions and he becomes implicated by his endeavours

ar.text: Giving up attachment to the fruits of his actions the materially unmotivated niṣkām karma-yogī attains constant السلام or liberation from actions and reactions But the fruitive worker sakām karmī is attached to the fruits of his actions and he becomes implicated by his endeavours

tr.text: Giving up attachment to the fruits of his actions the materially unmotivated niṣkām karma-yogī attains constant huzur or liberation from actions and reactions But the fruitive worker sakām karmī is attached to the fruits of his actions and he becomes implicated by his endeavours

sw.text: Giving up attachment to the fruits of his actions the materially unmotivated niṣkām karma-yogī attains constant amani or liberation from actions and reactions But the fruitive worker sakām karmī is attached to the fruits of his actions and he becomes implicated by his endeavours

he.text: Mentally renouncing all actions the sense-controlled embodied living being resides happily within the city of nine gates* neither acting nor causing work for others

ar.text: Mentally renouncing all actions the sense-controlled embodied living being resides happily within the city of nine gates* neither acting nor causing work for others

tr.text: Mentally renouncing all actions the sense-controlled embodied living being resides happily within the city of nine gates* neither acting nor causing work for others

sw.text: Mentally renouncing all actions the sense-controlled embodied living being resides happily within the city of nine gates* neither acting nor causing work for others

he.text: The Lord does not generate anyone’s ego of considering themselves ‘the doer’ nor their actions nor their association with the fruits of their actions These are all a result of their external nature of ignorance since time immemorial

ar.text: The Lord does not generate anyone’s ego of considering themselves ‘the doer’ nor their actions nor their association with the fruits of their actions These are all a result of their external nature of ignorance since time immemorial

tr.text: The Lord does not generate anyone’s ego of considering themselves ‘the doer’ nor their actions nor their association with the fruits of their actions These are all a result of their external nature of ignorance since time immemorial

sw.text: The Lord does not generate anyone’s ego of considering themselves ‘the doer’ nor their actions nor their association with the fruits of their actions These are all a result of their external nature of ignorance since time immemorial

he.text: The Supreme Lord accepts neither the sin nor the piety of anyone The living beings fall into illusion because their ידע is covered by ignorance

ar.text: The Supreme Lord accepts neither the sin nor the piety of anyone The living beings fall into illusion because their المعرفة is covered by ignorance

tr.text: The Supreme Lord accepts neither the sin nor the piety of anyone The living beings fall into illusion because their bilgi is covered by ignorance

sw.text: The Supreme Lord accepts neither the sin nor the piety of anyone The living beings fall into illusion because their ujuzi is covered by ignorance

he.text: For persons whose divine ידע has awakened their ignorance is destroyed and their חוכמה like the shining sun reveals the Supreme Reality

ar.text: For persons whose divine المعرفة has awakened their ignorance is destroyed and their الحكمة like the shining sun reveals the Supreme Reality

tr.text: For persons whose divine bilgi has awakened their ignorance is destroyed and their bilgelik like the shining sun reveals the Supreme Reality

sw.text: For persons whose divine ujuzi has awakened their ignorance is destroyed and their busara like the shining sun reveals the Supreme Reality

he.text: Those whose illusion has been completely dispelled by ידע always think of Me the Supreme Lord I am their מדיטציה and they abide in Me alone hearing and singing My unending glories in pure מסירות Thus they are liberated from the mundane

ar.text: Those whose illusion has been completely dispelled by المعرفة always think of Me the Supreme Lord I am their التأمل and they abide in Me alone hearing and singing My unending glories in pure التفاني Thus they are liberated from the mundane

tr.text: Those whose illusion has been completely dispelled by bilgi always think of Me the Supreme Lord I am their meditasyon and they abide in Me alone hearing and singing My unending glories in pure bağlılık Thus they are liberated from the mundane

sw.text: Those whose illusion has been completely dispelled by ujuzi always think of Me the Supreme Lord I am their kutafakari and they abide in Me alone hearing and singing My unending glories in pure ujitoaji Thus they are liberated from the mundane

he.text: The enlightened souls see transcendence within all living beings whether the humble and learned brāhmaṇ the cow the elephant the dog or the dog-eater Therefore they are to be known as paṇḍit—men of true חוכמה

ar.text: The enlightened souls see transcendence within all living beings whether the humble and learned brāhmaṇ the cow the elephant the dog or the dog-eater Therefore they are to be known as paṇḍit—men of true الحكمة

tr.text: The enlightened souls see transcendence within all living beings whether the humble and learned brāhmaṇ the cow the elephant the dog or the dog-eater Therefore they are to be known as paṇḍit—men of true bilgelik

sw.text: The enlightened souls see transcendence within all living beings whether the humble and learned brāhmaṇ the cow the elephant the dog or the dog-eater Therefore they are to be known as paṇḍit—men of true busara

he.text: Those whose minds are equipoised in Brahma have conquered the cycle of birth and death while living within this world By their perfect spiritual equanimity they are always situated in transcendence

ar.text: Those whose minds are equipoised in Brahma have conquered the cycle of birth and death while living within this world By their perfect spiritual equanimity they are always situated in transcendence

tr.text: Those whose minds are equipoised in Brahma have conquered the cycle of birth and death while living within this world By their perfect spiritual equanimity they are always situated in transcendence

sw.text: Those whose minds are equipoised in Brahma have conquered the cycle of birth and death while living within this world By their perfect spiritual equanimity they are always situated in transcendence

he.text: Absorbed in transcendence endowed with steady intelligence and free from the delusion of thinking of the body and associated objects as ‘me’ and ‘mine,’ the knower of the Absolute is neither happy when pleasant things come his way nor sad when unpleasant things come his way

ar.text: Absorbed in transcendence endowed with steady intelligence and free from the delusion of thinking of the body and associated objects as ‘me’ and ‘mine,’ the knower of the Absolute is neither happy when pleasant things come his way nor sad when unpleasant things come his way

tr.text: Absorbed in transcendence endowed with steady intelligence and free from the delusion of thinking of the body and associated objects as ‘me’ and ‘mine,’ the knower of the Absolute is neither happy when pleasant things come his way nor sad when unpleasant things come his way

sw.text: Absorbed in transcendence endowed with steady intelligence and free from the delusion of thinking of the body and associated objects as ‘me’ and ‘mine,’ the knower of the Absolute is neither happy when pleasant things come his way nor sad when unpleasant things come his way

he.text: Detaching his mind from external pleasures such a knower of the Absolute Truth tastes the inner joy of self-realisation Then absorbing himself in מדיטציה on the Absolute he experiences inexhaustible bliss

ar.text: Detaching his mind from external pleasures such a knower of the Absolute Truth tastes the inner joy of self-realisation Then absorbing himself in التأمل on the Absolute he experiences inexhaustible bliss

tr.text: Detaching his mind from external pleasures such a knower of the Absolute Truth tastes the inner joy of self-realisation Then absorbing himself in meditasyon on the Absolute he experiences inexhaustible bliss

sw.text: Detaching his mind from external pleasures such a knower of the Absolute Truth tastes the inner joy of self-realisation Then absorbing himself in kutafakari on the Absolute he experiences inexhaustible bliss

he.text: O son of Kuntī the pleasures that arise from the contact of the senses with their objects are the cause of unhappiness as they are transient The wise never delight in such pleasures

ar.text: O son of Kuntī the pleasures that arise from the contact of the senses with their objects are the cause of unhappiness as they are transient The wise never delight in such pleasures

tr.text: O son of Kuntī the pleasures that arise from the contact of the senses with their objects are the cause of unhappiness as they are transient The wise never delight in such pleasures

sw.text: O son of Kuntī the pleasures that arise from the contact of the senses with their objects are the cause of unhappiness as they are transient The wise never delight in such pleasures

he.text: Know that one who before leaving the body utilises the opportunity of this life to check the impulses of desire and anger—he is a yogī who knows true אושר

ar.text: Know that one who before leaving the body utilises the opportunity of this life to check the impulses of desire and anger—he is a yogī who knows true السعادة

tr.text: Know that one who before leaving the body utilises the opportunity of this life to check the impulses of desire and anger—he is a yogī who knows true mutluluk

sw.text: Know that one who before leaving the body utilises the opportunity of this life to check the impulses of desire and anger—he is a yogī who knows true furaha

he.text: Such a yogī sees the self within and delights in the bliss of the self within Attaining the transcendental state he attains liberation from matter and entry into the abode of Brahma

ar.text: Such a yogī sees the self within and delights in the bliss of the self within Attaining the transcendental state he attains liberation from matter and entry into the abode of Brahma

tr.text: Such a yogī sees the self within and delights in the bliss of the self within Attaining the transcendental state he attains liberation from matter and entry into the abode of Brahma

sw.text: Such a yogī sees the self within and delights in the bliss of the self within Attaining the transcendental state he attains liberation from matter and entry into the abode of Brahma

he.text: Sinless free from doubt self-controlled and dedicated to the welfare of all living beings the seers of truth attain such liberation

ar.text: Sinless free from doubt self-controlled and dedicated to the welfare of all living beings the seers of truth attain such liberation

tr.text: Sinless free from doubt self-controlled and dedicated to the welfare of all living beings the seers of truth attain such liberation

sw.text: Sinless free from doubt self-controlled and dedicated to the welfare of all living beings the seers of truth attain such liberation

he.text: Persons of the renounced order who have controlled their thoughts who are free from desire and anger and who have achieved ידע of the nature of the soul are liberated whether they live or die

ar.text: Persons of the renounced order who have controlled their thoughts who are free from desire and anger and who have achieved المعرفة of the nature of the soul are liberated whether they live or die

tr.text: Persons of the renounced order who have controlled their thoughts who are free from desire and anger and who have achieved bilgi of the nature of the soul are liberated whether they live or die

sw.text: Persons of the renounced order who have controlled their thoughts who are free from desire and anger and who have achieved ujuzi of the nature of the soul are liberated whether they live or die

he.text: I am the enjoyer and objective of the sacrifices performed by persons of action and of the austerities performed by persons of ידע I am Nārāyaṇ the indwelling monitor of all planes of life the worshippable Supreme Personality who awards liberation I am Kṛṣṇa the well-wisher of all and the dear friend of the devotees One who knows Me thus attains the bliss of knowing his own original divine identity

ar.text: I am the enjoyer and objective of the sacrifices performed by persons of action and of the austerities performed by persons of المعرفة I am Nārāyaṇ the indwelling monitor of all planes of life the worshippable Supreme Personality who awards liberation I am Kṛṣṇa the well-wisher of all and the dear friend of the devotees One who knows Me thus attains the bliss of knowing his own original divine identity

tr.text: I am the enjoyer and objective of the sacrifices performed by persons of action and of the austerities performed by persons of bilgi I am Nārāyaṇ the indwelling monitor of all planes of life the worshippable Supreme Personality who awards liberation I am Kṛṣṇa the well-wisher of all and the dear friend of the devotees One who knows Me thus attains the bliss of knowing his own original divine identity

sw.text: I am the enjoyer and objective of the sacrifices performed by persons of action and of the austerities performed by persons of ujuzi I am Nārāyaṇ the indwelling monitor of all planes of life the worshippable Supreme Personality who awards liberation I am Kṛṣṇa the well-wisher of all and the dear friend of the devotees One who knows Me thus attains the bliss of knowing his own original divine identity
